Guideline: Use file-based routing as the navigation source of truth
Description: Expo Router works best when the app directory defines the route map
Do: Create screens by adding files under app
Define stacks tabs or drawers in _layout.tsx
Don't: Repeat navigator setup in every screen

Guideline: Use Link and router APIs intentionally
Description: Prefer Expo Router primitives for navigation

Guideline: Use groups to organize without changing URLs
Do: Group tabs auth or settings screens

Description: Keep route params explicit and predictable

Guideline: Lean on deep links and shareable routes
Description: Expo Router gives route-based linking out of the box
Do: Design screen structure with shareable URLs
Don't: Hide app state behind non-addressable navigation

Guideline: Keep screens light and split naturally
Description: File-based routes help lazy evaluation and bundling
Do: Keep heavy logic away from navigator shells
Code Bad: All app logic in root layout

Description: Use native stacks tabs and gestures where appropriate
Do: Choose stacks/tabs that fit mobile tasks
Don't: Force web-like nav patterns on every flow

Description: Screen data should match route responsibility
Do: Fetch or derive data per screen

Guideline: Gate access with groups and layout logic
Description: Centralize auth redirects near route boundaries
Don't: Scatter redirect checks in leaf screens

Guideline: Remember Expo Router spans web too
Description: Keep route naming links and layouts friendly for universal apps
Do: Test both native and web navigation

Description: Safer href construction reduces broken links
Do: Enable typed routes and lean on autocomplete

Description: Form submission UX should reflect mobile realities
Do: Show pending state and error recovery

Guideline: Prefer clear route names over clever nesting
Description: Developers should understand the app map from the tree

Guideline: Use platform-specific files only when needed
Description: Stay universal until native differences are real
